results.possibleIdentity: Michael Kirby was a New York-based artist, critic, and scholar who was a central figure in the avant-garde art scene of the 1960s and 1970s. He is widely recognized for his work documenting and theorizing 'Happenings,' notably through his book 'Happenings: An Illustrated Anthology' (1965) and 'The Art of Time: Essays on the Avant-Garde' (1969). Kirby was deeply involved with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) and the landmark 1966 event '9 Evenings: Theatre and Engineering' at the 69th Regiment Armory in New York. During '9 Evenings,' he served as both a documentarian and a participant, specifically assisting with the remote-controlled carts in Deborah Hay's performance 'Solo.' In the early 1970s, he became the editor of 'The Drama Review' (TDR), where he promoted the practice of 'straight documentation' for performance art. His work as an artist included conceptual and photographic projects, and he was featured in the 1970 portfolio 'Artists & Photographs' alongside contemporary figures such as Bruce Nauman and Andy Warhol.
